S1_signed_zero.verdict: NON-CANONICAL: +0.0 and -0.0 are the same number but yield different commitments. Any reimplementation/platform that produces -0.0 vs +0.0 in a cell breaks verification.

S2_nan_payloads.verdict: Distinct NaN bit-patterns are all 'NaN' yet commit differently; and NaN-valued cells make verify() self-inconsistent since NaN != NaN. Kernel does not emit NaN for finite seeds (a1), so severity is conditional.

S3_endianness_dual_schema.verdict: The SAME terminal field has two project-sanctioned serializations (WLv2 little-endian tobytes vs WLv3 big-endian '>f8'), hence two distinct commitments. Commitment identity depends on schema label, not just psi*.

S4_energy_block_redundancy.verdict: The 32-byte packed energy block is a deterministic function of psi*, adding no second-preimage strength beyond psi* itself, while adding 4 more float64 channels that must match bit-exactly across implementations (amplifying the a6 reproducibility problem).

injectivity.note: No two distinct bit-pattern fields share a serialization; the ambiguity is the reverse — one logical field, many byte images.
